Question: Calculate the derivative $$d y \text{/} d x$$ for the plane curve defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = t^{2} - 4 t , y ( t ) = 2 t^{3} - 6 t , -2 \le t \le 3$$ and locate any critical points on its graph.
Answer: $$x^{\prime} ( t ) = 2 t - 4$$ and $$y^{\prime} ( t ) = 6 t^{2} - 6 ,$$ so $$\frac{d y}{d x} = \frac{6 t^{2} - 6}{2 t - 4} = \frac{3 t^{2} - 3}{t - 2} .$$ This expression is undefined when $$t = 2$$ and equal to zero when $$t = ±1 .$$

Question: Find the equation of the tangent line to the curve defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = t^{2} - 4 t , y ( t ) = 2 t^{3} - 6 t , -2 \le t \le 10 \text{when} t = 5 .$$
Answer: The equation of the tangent line is $$y = 24 x + 100 .$$

Question: Calculate the second derivative $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ for the plane curve defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = t^{2} - 4 t , y ( t ) = 2 t^{3} - 6 t , -2 \le t \le 3$$ and locate any critical points on its graph.
Find the area under the curve of the hypocycloid defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = 3 \text{cos} t + \text{cos} 3 t , y ( t ) = 3 \text{sin} t - \text{sin} 3 t , 0 \le t \le \pi .$$
Answer: $$A = 3 \pi$$ (Note that the integral formula actually yields a negative answer. This is due to the fact that $$x ( t )$$ is a decreasing function over the interval $$[ 0 , 2 \pi ] ;$$ that is, the curve is traced from right to left.)

Question: Find the arc length of the curve defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = 3 t^{2} , y ( t ) = 2 t^{3} , 1 \le t \le 3 .$$
Find the surface area generated when the plane curve defined by the equations $$x ( t ) = t^{3} , y ( t ) = t^{2} , 0 \le t \le 1$$ is revolved around the x -axis.
